req.title: Close encounter (boundary)
req.statement: A clinician shall close an Encounter; closed Encounters shall reject further modifications except for addendum Observations. Boundary cases on timestamps and numeric elements must be tested.

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-032 covers three orthogonal risk areas: (1) the happy-path state transition open→finished, (2) rejection of modifications on a closed encounter (error pathway), and (3) boundary values on timestamp and numeric fields at the moment of closing. STC drives the state-transition test, NEG drives the 409-rejection test, BVA drives the timestamp/numeric boundary test, DT captures the addendum-observation exception that closed encounters must still accept, and NEG (not EP) is used for the fifth test because it exercises a negative scenario (non-addendum observation rejected on a closed encounter). The violation in test[4] was that a negative kind was paired with EP; EP is an equivalence-partitioning heuristic appropriate for positive or neutral partition tests, not for negative rejection scenarios. Changing heuristic to NEG for test[4] corrects the logical inconsistency while preserving all other tests unchanged.

tests.name: test_close_encounter_sets_status_finished
preconditions: A valid Encounter exists in status=in-progress
Clinician auth token is available via <auth_fixture>
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_close_encounter_sets_status_finished(api_client, open_encounter):
    enc_id = open_encounter["id"]

    resp = api_client.post(f"/Encounter/{enc_id}/close")
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "finished"

    get_resp = api_client.get(f"/Encounter/{enc_id}")
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    assert get_resp.json()["status"] == "finished"

tests.name: test_modify_closed_encounter_returns_409
preconditions: A valid Encounter exists and has already been closed (status=finished)
Clinician auth token is available via <auth_fixture>
actions: Attempt PATCH /Encounter/{id} to update a non-addendum field (e.g. subject reference)
Capture the HTTP response status and error body
expected: Response body contains an error indicating the encounter is closed
trace: R-HLT-032: error pathway 409 when modifying closed encounter
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_modify_closed_encounter_returns_409(api_client, closed_encounter):
    enc_id = closed_encounter["id"]
    payload = {"subject": {"reference": "Patient/different-patient"}}

    resp = api_client.patch(
        f"/Encounter/{enc_id}",
        json=payload
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 409
    error_body = resp.json()
    assert "closed" in str(error_body).lower() or "finished" in str(error_body).lower()

tests.name: test_close_encounter_boundary_timestamp_and_numeric
preconditions: An Encounter exists in status=in-progress with period.start set to a known value
The close payload accepts an explicit end timestamp and a numeric duration element
actions: POST /Encounter/{id}/close with period.end equal to period.start (zero-duration, lower boundary)
POST /Encounter/{id2}/close with period.end = period.start + 1 second (just-above boundary)
POST /Encounter/{id4}/close with numeric duration = MAX_INT (upper boundary)
expected: Zero-duration close (period.end == period.start) returns HTTP 200 or documented validation error
One-second duration close returns HTTP 200 and status=finished
duration=0 returns HTTP 200 or documented validation code
duration=MAX_INT returns HTTP 200 or HTTP 422 per server capacity limits; must not return 500
trace: R-HLT-032: boundary cases on timestamps and numeric elements must be tested
tests.executable:
import pytest
import sys

def test_close_encounter_boundary_timestamp_and_numeric(
    api_client, open_encounter_factory
):
    base_start = "2024-01-15T08:00:00Z"

    # BVA-1: period.end == period.start (zero duration)
    enc1 = open_encounter_factory(start=base_start)
    r1 = api_client.post(
        f"/Encounter/{enc1['id']}/close",
        json={"period": {"end": base_start}}
    )
    assert r1.status_code in (200, 422), f"Unexpected {r1.status_code}"

    # BVA-2: period.end = start + 1 second (just-above lower boundary)
    enc2 = open_encounter_factory(start=base_start)
    r2 = api_client.post(
        f"/Encounter/{enc2['id']}/close",
        json={"period": {"end": "2024-01-15T08:00:01Z"}}
    )
    assert r2.status_code == 200
    assert r2.json()["status"] == "finished"

    # BVA-3: numeric duration = 0
    enc3 = open_encounter_factory(start=base_start)
    r3 = api_client.post(
        f"/Encounter/{enc3['id']}/close",
        json={"length": {"value": 0, "unit": "min"}}
    )
    assert r3.status_code in (200, 422), f"Unexpected {r3.status_code}"

    # BVA-4: numeric duration = MAX_INT (upper boundary)
    enc4 = open_encounter_factory(start=base_start)
    r4 = api_client.post(
        f"/Encounter/{enc4['id']}/close",
        json={"length": {"value": sys.maxsize, "unit": "min"}}
    )
    assert r4.status_code != 500, "Server must not crash on MAX_INT duration"

tests.name: test_addendum_observation_allowed_on_closed_encounter
preconditions: A valid Encounter exists with status=finished
actions: POST /Observation with encounter reference pointing to the closed encounter and category=addendum
Retrieve the Observation to confirm persistence
expected: Observation is persisted and linked to the finished Encounter
trace: R-HLT-032: closed Encounters shall reject further modifications EXCEPT for addendum Observations
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_addendum_observation_allowed_on_closed_encounter(
    api_client, closed_encounter
):
    enc_id = closed_encounter["id"]
    obs_payload = {
        "resourceType": "Observation",
        "status": "final",
        "category": [{"coding": [{"code": "addendum"}]}],
        "encounter": {"reference": f"Encounter/{enc_id}"},
        "code": {"coding": [{"system": "http://loinc.org", "code": "55423-8"}]},
        "valueString": "Post-close addendum note"
    }

    create_resp = api_client.post("/Observation", json=obs_payload)
    assert create_resp.status_code == 201, (
        f"Addendum Observation rejected: {create_resp.status_code}"
    )

    obs_id = create_resp.json()["id"]
    get_resp = api_client.get(f"/Observation/{obs_id}")
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    enc_ref = get_resp.json()["encounter"]["reference"]
    assert enc_ref == f"Encounter/{enc_id}"

tests.name: test_non_addendum_observation_rejected_on_closed_encounter
preconditions: A valid Encounter exists with status=finished
actions: POST /Observation with encounter reference pointing to the closed encounter without addendum category
expected: POST returns HTTP 409 or HTTP 422 (non-addendum write blocked)
trace: R-HLT-032: closed Encounters shall reject further modifications except for addendum Observations
R-HLT-032: error pathway 409 when modifying closed encounter
